name: Cybersecurity Management Guidelines
owner: Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry + Information-technology Promotion Agency, Japan (METI + IPA)
description: Ver. 3.0, issued March 2023. Executive/CISO-level cybersecurity governance framework: 3 principles for management plus 10 important items for CISOs (risk management, resourcing, supply-chain security, incident response, stakeholder communication).
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/meti.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain. Jointly issued with IPA, a quasi-public implementing agency -- moderate rather than high confidence.

current_extraction.notes: Full English text acquired. meti.go.jp returns HTTP 403 to plain curl/WebFetch (bot/WAF protection) but a real Playwright browser session succeeded (HTTP 200) -- fetched the PDF bytes via an in-page fetch() call and saved via base64 round-trip.
